- **Signs of editing/manipulation:** None obvious. The typography, spacing, highlighting (“possible sea mines” in yellow), and link styling look consistent with a normal webpage/news post. No visible cut-and-paste seams, warped text, or mismatched fonts.
- **Signs of synthetic generation:** Unlikely. AI-generated images often show garbled text or inconsistent letterforms; here the text is crisp and coherent throughout.
- **Counterfactual/scene inconsistencies:** Not applicable visually, since it’s just text on a page. The image itself doesn’t provide independent visual evidence about the real-world claim.
- **Overall:** The image looks **authentic as a screenshot/document capture**, though screenshots can be fabricated by editing HTML or recreating a page—nothing in the pixels alone conclusively proves provenance.
